title: AttentionReceiptV2
description: I-010E AttentionReceiptV2@2 — one immutable stage record of the request-correlated receipt stream, encoding each stage's selected telemetry verbatim (FR-014, c834e8c): observation records the schema version, trigger and continuity-scope IDs, snapshot sizes, coverage, and included event IDs; attention records classifier and effective dispositions, classifier identity, evidence, transition-valve facts, and the effective policy's inspectable provenance, or the bypass fact `classifier_not_invoked` with cause and trusted policy provenance, with operational error kept separate and itself carrying an explicit operator wake-action override and its provenance when one applied; participant-host records the wake source, packet sizes, delivered event IDs, expansion calls, invocation, and the observed sent/silent/unknown outcome; transport records delivery hygiene, routing/send facts, and transport errors. The canonical order is observation -> attention -> participant-host -> transport; a prefix-partial receipt is valid-in-progress. The stage-to-writer binding is part of this public per-record contract (FR-010, rejection R3): the closed map is observation -> observation-provider, attention -> attention-engine, participant-host -> participant-host, transport -> transport, and a record attributing one stage to another stage's owner is invalid as a single document. The multi-record rules — append-only sequence, canonical order, immutability, request-ID correlation, and stream-level writer ownership — span multiple records and remain runtime-adapter-only in addition (FR-012 receipt-stage sequence class). Unknown and unavailable remain explicit. @2 (post-acceptance amendment A1, evidence/v2/attention/dependency-010-post-acceptance-blocker.md) adds the classifier-outcome body's required `policy_provenance` and the error body's conditional `wake_action`/`policy_provenance` pair: the selected design requires the effective policy and its source to be inspectable in receipts, and an operator's explicit `NO_WAKE` override to the shared `WAKE` default to be separately receipted as operational failure policy — neither was representable in @1.

oneOf.$comment: Classifier outcome with effective routing (transition-valve facts), classifier identity/evidence, and the effective policy's inspectable provenance (@2 amendment A1: c834e8c 'the effective policy and its source are inspectable in receipts').

Operational error, kept separate from any social outcome. wake_action is the closed override discriminator, exactly 'NO_WAKE' (rejection A1-R1, review-2026-07-19-v2-integrator-amendment-A1.md: WAKE is the shared default, never itself a receipted override, so it is not a permitted member); wake_action and policy_provenance are present together exactly when that explicit operator override applied (@2 amendment A1: c834e8c 'NO_WAKE is an explicit operator override... receipted as operational failure policy'); their absence means the default WAKE behavior applied with no override to receipt.
Trusted bypass: no classifier was invoked and no social result is fabricated.

participantHostBody.$comment: Participant silence is a distinct staged outcome — separate from model suppression and from non-invocation — with no handled/owed/obligation state. The observed outcome is sent/silent/unknown (FR-014), not a handled/owed social state.

allOf.$comment: FR-010 per-record stage-to-writer binding: each stage is written only by its single directly observing owner; a cross-owner record is invalid as a single document.
